input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. Their XAML has `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but the labels are blank even though the ViewModel has values.

The most common cause: they haven't set the `BindingContext` of the page to their ViewModel instance.

Another possible cause: the ViewModel properties don't raise `PropertyChanged` notifications (i.e., they don't implement `INotifyPropertyChanged` or use `ObservableProperty`).

But the most likely issue given the symptoms ("properties have values" but labels are blank) is that `BindingContext` is not set.


I should keep my answer focused and direct since this is a straightforward troubleshooting question.
